articles.title: Git Worktrees Are Great
articles.excerpt: I want to tell you about a tool I've been using for a few months now, that I can't believe that I ever went without. Recently, I've been more productive, experimental and I've learned more about my de…
articles.body: I want to tell you about a tool I've been using for a few months now, that I can't believe that I ever went without. Recently, I've been more productive, experimental and I've learned more about my development environment just from using this tool. Alright, I'm done burying the lead. The tool is git worktrees . Worktrees is a feature built into git that allows you to have multiple working trees of a repository at once. That means you can have multiple branches checked out, with incomplete changes on each one. Instead of having one repository where your manipulation happens, you can have infinite copies (or, as many copies as you can hold on a hard drive)! Often, I am in the middle of working on a feature and I get an email about an urgent bug or a small change that needs made immediately.
articles.author: Nolan Miller

articles.excerpt: The Xtra Muse 2 Pro. | Image; Xtra After selling a barely disguised version of the hit DJI Osmo Pocket 3 in the United States last year, Xtra Technology seemed ready to sneak in its version of the new…
articles.body: The Xtra Muse 2 Pro. | Image; Xtra After selling a barely disguised version of the hit DJI Osmo Pocket 3 in the United States last year, Xtra Technology seemed ready to sneak in its version of the new dual-lens Osmo Pocket 4 Pro, too. But today, Xtra has abruptly removed the new Xtra Muse 2 Pro from its website landing page, is pausing preorders indefinitely, and says it'll refund all the $20 deposits that it's already collected. Just days after showing off the camera at a Los Angeles launch event , the company is no longer necessarily committing to launch at all: "XTRA has not made a final announcement regarding future product availability. articles.title: Samsung may finally let you set screen zoom levels on a per-app basis
articles.excerpt: Samsung's latest foldables are hiding a big new accessibility feature.
articles.body: TL;DR Android’s screen zoom settings currently impact all apps equally. New Samsung firmware reveals work towards separate zoom settings for each app. Android users already have a nice assortment of options for making it easier to see what their apps are doing. We can adjust text size, magnify specific areas of the screen , or just zoom everything at once. The problem is, not all apps are created equally, and some may look better at different levels of zoom than others. Thankfully, Samsung appears to be working on an easy solution. You’re reading an Authority Insights story. Authority Insights brings you all the latest exclusive reports, app teardowns, leaks, and in-depth tech coverage from the Android Authority team that you won’t find anywhere else.

articles.title: I Stopped Doing the Work Myself. My OpenClaw Agent Now Delegates to Sub-Agents.
articles.excerpt: There's a moment when you stop thinking of your AI agent as a tool you're holding, and start thinking of it as infrastructure you're operating. For me, that moment was when I started delegating work t…
articles.body: There's a moment when you stop thinking of your AI agent as a tool you're holding, and start thinking of it as infrastructure you're operating. For me, that moment was when I started delegating work to sub-agents — spawning child sessions inside my main OpenClaw agent to handle parallel streams of work. It changed how I think about the whole system. Instead of one agent doing things sequentially, I had a small agency of agents, each doing one thing well. This is what that actually looks like in practice. The Problem With a Single Agent When you run one agent doing everything, you hit a ceiling fast. The agent can only do one thing at a time, in one context.
articles.author: MrClaw207

articles.title: 'The Silent Cartographer' skulls and terminal locations in Halo Campaign Evolved
articles.excerpt: All the skull and terminal locations on "The Silent Cartographer" level in Halo: Campaign Evolved.
articles.body: "The Silent Cartographer," one of the most iconic Halo missions of all time, has three skulls and one terminal to seek out in Halo Campaign Evolved . This is the mission where Master Chief is in search of the map for the entirety of the halo, after successfully rescuing Captain Keyes in " Truth and Reconciliation ," but getting his hands on it is easier said than done. It's also one of the best looking levels in the remake, with an absolutely stunning beach encircling the island.
articles.author: Ford James

articles.title: Token != Stateless
articles.excerpt: What a Mobile Developer Learned Diving Into Cookies, Sessions, and Auth A React Native developer's journey from "how do I keep a user logged in?" to the one distinction that made web auth finally clic…
articles.body: What a Mobile Developer Learned Diving Into Cookies, Sessions, and Auth A React Native developer's journey from "how do I keep a user logged in?" to the one distinction that made web auth finally click, plus a strong (and debatable) opinion on when to use each. Back in 2022, I got an opportunity I am still grateful for: working on a real, live React Native project at my previous organization. It was the kind of project you learn a lot on. Along the way, as I picked up TypeScript, I also got my hands dirty with backend work in Node, and I ended up shipping a few backend projects I was genuinely proud of. I never fully let that go. Even after moving on, I kept building small and medium side projects in Node, partly to stay sharp and partly because I enjoy it.
articles.author: Jishant Sethi

articles.title: The Six Flavors of Dependency Injection in Flutter
articles.excerpt: Understanding the full spectrum of state location, service retrieval, and IoC in Dart & Flutter Dependency Injection (DI) is often discussed as if it were a single pattern or package. In Flutter, deve…
articles.body: Understanding the full spectrum of state location, service retrieval, and IoC in Dart & Flutter Dependency Injection (DI) is often discussed as if it were a single pattern or package. In Flutter, developers frequently debate get_it vs Provider vs Riverpod as if they are competing for the exact same job. In reality, Dependency Injection in Flutter exists on a spectrum . There are six distinct "flavors" of dependency injection and service location in Dart and Flutter—ranging from basic compile-time parameters to advanced code-generated IoC containers. Understanding all six flavors allows you to choose the simplest, safest mechanism for each layer of your application. Flavor 1: Constructor Injection (Direct Passing) The simplest and purest form of DI in Dart.
articles.author: Randal L. Schwartz

articles.body: raw | Photo: Dominic Preston / The Verge In response to a report that Nothing is planning to "exit 12 markets as global shipments decline," Nothing cofounder Akis Evangelidis said the company is "reorganizing" and laying off some of its staff, but that "the reported numbers are way overblown." Evangelidis disputed a claim about underwhelming sales for Nothing's Phone 4B, saying it "sold 29,537 units on Day 1 only, breaking records in its price segment." He also said Nothing isn't shutting down in any markets, and is instead "consolidating" its operations into regional hubs as well as introducing an "AI-native" business unit. The update comes as rising component prices squeeze the … Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Why Cognition bought Poke: AI personality is becoming a competitive advantage
articles.excerpt: AI coding startup Cognition has acquired Poke, the AI assistant you text like a friend, in a deal valuing the startup in the low nine figures. The acquisition brings Poke’s conversational style and in…
articles.body: AI coding startup Cognition has acquired Poke, the AI assistant you text like a friend, in a deal valuing the startup in the low nine figures. The acquisition brings Poke’s conversational style and interaction model to Cognition’s coding agent Devin, reflecting a growing belief that how AI assistants interact with users is as important as the models powering them.

articles.title: Is the PlayStation Network down for you? Here’s what’s going on
articles.excerpt: Every service on the PSN is having issues.
articles.body: Credit: Oliver Cragg / Android Authority TL;DR PlayStation Network is currently down. Sony says that all of its services are experiencing issues. This includes account management, gaming and social, video content, the PS Store, and PS Direct. If you’re having problems with the PlayStation Network, know that there’s nothing wrong with your setup. Any issues you’re experiencing are on Sony’s side. The PlayStation Network is currently experiencing an outage. According to the PlayStation status page , every service is experiencing problems at the moment. This includes account management, gaming and social, video content, the PS Store, and PS Direct. As such, the company warns that you might have trouble signing in, editing account details, streaming games, making purchases, and so on.

articles.body: The Lucasfilm Publishing panel at San Diego Comic-Con was, not too surprisingly, filled with announcements about upcoming Star Wars projects from Marvel. Unexpectedly, however, the panel also revealed that Indiana Jones would be getting his first Marvel Comics series in over three decades.

articles.title: Microsoft should make these 10 Xbox games backwards-compatible on PC
articles.excerpt: With select Xbox games coming to the PC platform from Microsoft, here are 10 games that deserve a fresh chance to delight players.
articles.body: Sometimes, it’s easy to forget how diverse the original Xbox game library really was. Early in its life, a handful of major Japanese publishers released some absolute bangers. Sega was surprisingly prolific, Tecmo truly came into its own, and Microsoft itself worked with skilled partners to develop a variety of hidden gems. Other third parties from around the world made terrific contributions that ensured there was always something new and compelling to play. Soon, it could be easy to revisit this halcyon era — or at least parts of it.
articles.author: Jason Venter

articles.title: We boldly chat to 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' showrunners about dinosaurs in space and Lovecraftian horrors for season 4 (interview)
articles.excerpt: 'The minute we started talking about doing dinosaurs, we were like, 'Yeah, let’s figure out a way to do that.''
articles.body: Henry Alonso Myers and Akiva Goldsman are the primary ringleaders behind " Star Trek: Strange New Worlds ", and they're about to unleash the next cluster of 10 Season 4 episodes for their spirited space show. With an abundance of genre-crossing indulgences that are scary, surreal, and sometimes silly, the Paramount+ hit is operating at full warp speed in this penultimate outing, the debut episode of which just premiered on July 23, 2026. We hitched a ride with the acclaimed executive producers to learn all about their vision for "Strange New Worlds'" fourth season and why they believe it will prove to be the finest one yet, taking cues from ‘90s-style time-travel odysseys, haunted starships, noir crime thrillers, puppet mishaps, and utopian fantasies.
articles.author: Jeff Spry

articles.title: NASA’s ESCAPADE Snaps Family Portrait of Earth, Moon
articles.excerpt: On July 3, one of NASA’s two Mars-destined ESCAPADE (Escape and Plasma Acceleration and Dynamics Explorers) spacecraft captured photos of Earth and the Moon in visible and thermal infrared light. At t…
articles.body: On July 3, one of NASA’s two Mars-destined ESCAPADE (Escape and Plasma Acceleration and Dynamics Explorers) spacecraft captured photos of Earth and the Moon in visible and thermal infrared light. At the time, the spacecraft was 363,250 miles (584,600 kilometers) from Earth and 115,600 miles (186,100 kilometers) from the Moon, making the Moon appear relatively large. Taken with the Sun only partly illuminating Earth and the Moon, the visible light image shows the two bodies as crescents, with only around 8% of each face sunlit. Yet in the thermal infrared image, the shadowed hemisphere of Earth is illuminated by its own heat from both the atmosphere and surface, glowing at minus 10 to minus 44 degrees Fahrenheit (250 to 280 kelvins).
articles.author: NASA Breaking News

articles.title: Nothing rejects market exit rumors, insists global operations remain active
articles.excerpt: Nothing is firing back at a report alleging its existing 12 markets.
articles.body: Credit: Damien Wilde / Android Authority TL;DR Nothing’s co-founder denies a recent report that the company is leaving 12 markets. He states that the company is going through a reorganization to operate more efficiently. Akis Evangelidis also says that the Phone 4b sold 29,537 units on the first day alone. OnePlus recently confirmed it would be leaving North America and Europe. A new report claims that OnePlus won’t be the only phone company looking to reduce its footprint this year. The report alleges that London-based manufacturer Nothing is planning to exit 12 markets. Now the company’s co-founder has come forward, strongly denying the claims made in the report. Nothing co-founder Akis Evangelidis has taken to X (formerly Twitter) to respond to the report in question.
articles.author: Ryan McNeal

articles.title: The BOOX Picco could be the tiny e-reader of your dreams
articles.excerpt: It's got a 3.97" display and expandable storage.
articles.body: TL;DR BOOX has teased it’s upcoming Picco e-reader. The Picco features a 3.97″ ePaper display and expandable storage. Pricing and availability have not been announced. The BOOX Palma 2 is a great Kindle alternative for readers who want something a little more pocketable. Today, though, BOOX is teasing an even more portable e-reader. The BOOX Picco comes with a truly tiny 3.97″ ePaper screen, and it’ll be offered at an “accessible price point.” We don’t know much about the Picco yet. It’ll come with a front-lit, sub-4″ monochrome screen, and it’ll offer expandable storage via microSD cards. At first blush, the Picco looks quite a bit like the similarly small Xteink X4 , which has a 4.3″ display.
articles.author: Taylor Kerns

articles.title: Prime Video's biggest sci-fi show of the year redefines a cyberpunk icon
articles.excerpt: Amazon just revealed the first trailer and release date for its Blade Runner 2099 at San Diego Comic-Con.
articles.body: “We already fought a war, we lost.” The first official trailer for Blade Runner 2099 starts with a bang, revealing how much has changed in the world first brought to life by director Ridley Scott since his trailblazing 1982 movie Blade Runner. That film, itself an adaptation of Philip K. Dick's seminal novel Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?, imagined a neon-soaked noir dystopia of 2019 — and popularized the cyberpunk genre in cinema along the way.
articles.author: Jake Kleinman

articles.title: Cape Fear Episode 9 Recap and Review – ‘The Scar’
articles.excerpt: The penultimate episode of Apple TV's Cape Fear suffers from rushed resolutions and bland storytelling. Read our full recap and review of Episode 9, "The Scar."
articles.body: Spoilers below for Episode 9 of Cape Fear . New episodes stream every Friday on Apple TV. Cape Fear Episode 9 is a mess. Whereas the last few entries of the show have been propulsive and mind-bogglingly chaotic, the latest episode of the Apple TV series - titled “The Scar” - commits the cardinal sin of being boring. Sure, there are a few wild moments (No Natalie! Don’t have sex with your maybe-sister in prison in order to steal her hidden cell phone!), but “The Scar” is otherwise a featherweight filler episode premiering right when the series needs to be firing on all cylinders. When we last saw Anna , she was driving like a bat out of hell away from Savannah to find Max’s sister Crystal in North Carolina.
articles.author: Michael Peyton

articles.title: Woot Has Great Deals on Factory Reconditioned MSI Gaming PCs Thanks to a New 22% Off Coupon
articles.excerpt: Prebuilt desktop computers equipped with RTX 50 series GPUs for way below market price.
articles.body: Woot, which is owned by Amazon, is clearing out its inventory of factory refurbished MSI gaming PCs by offering an additional 22% off discount with coupon code " BIRTHDAY26 ". Amazon Prime members get free shipping, but even if you're not a member you only pay $4.99. These prebuilts are in "like new" condition and come with a 6 month MSI warranty. Extra 22% Off MSI Gaming PCs at Woot RTX 5080 Gaming PC for $2,223 This system is equipped with an AMD Ryzen 9 9900X CPU, GeForce RTX 5080 GPU, 32GB of RAM, and a 2TB SSD. The RTX 5080 is one of the fastest cards on the market, bested only by the $2,000 $4,000 RTX 5090 and the discontinued RTX 4090.

articles.excerpt: Weeks after Anthropic's latest toe-to-toe with the US government, and days after an OpenAI security incident that dominated tech industry discussions, Anthropic on Thursday released its newest model,…
articles.body: Weeks after Anthropic's latest toe-to-toe with the US government, and days after an OpenAI security incident that dominated tech industry discussions, Anthropic on Thursday released its newest model, Claude Opus 5. The company said in a release that Opus 5 "comes close to the capabilities of Claude Fable 5 in many domains" and is much better at complex coding tasks. (Fable 5 is the public-facing Mythos-class model that drew the government's ire, was taken offline for a few weeks along with Mythos 5, and then brought back with even stronger cyber safeguards than before.) The Fable 5 concerns - and the ensuing weeks of negotiations between … Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: New Crew Members Welcomed to International Space Station
articles.excerpt: From left, Expedition 74 flight engineers Anna Kikina and Pyotr Dubrov of Roscosmos, and Anil Menon of NASA pose for a July 18, 2026, photo while holding a cake celebrating their recent arrival aboard…
articles.body: NASA/Chris Williams From left, Expedition 74 flight engineers Anna Kikina and Pyotr Dubrov of Roscosmos, and Anil Menon of NASA pose for a July 18, 2026, photo while holding a cake celebrating their recent arrival aboard the International Space Station. The trio arrived at the space station on July 14, 2026, after launching from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan earlier the same day. Kikina, Dubrov, and Menon are in the second week of their planned eight-and-a-half-month mission. They are using their new skills to conduct space research while still familiarizing themselves with living and working in space. Keep up with space station activity on the International Space Station blog. Image credit: NASA/Chris Williams
articles.author: HQ Web Team

articles.title: NASA Announces New Spacecraft Technology Demonstration Mission at Moon
articles.excerpt: NASA is working with industry to advance the next phase of cislunar infrastructure for the agency’s Artemis program and Moon Base, including orbital assets and demonstrations. Under a contract awarded…
articles.body: 4 min read Preparations for Next Moonwalk Simulations Underway (and Underwater) An artist’s rendering of NASA’s CAPSTONE 02 spacecraft in lunar orbit. The mission features two identical small spacecraft that will further mature technologies to support Artemis, Moon Base, and deep space exploration. Terran Orbital NASA is working with industry to advance the next phase of cislunar infrastructure for the agency’s Artemis program and Moon Base, including orbital assets and demonstrations.
articles.author: Loura Hall

articles.title: What lurks beneath the volcanoes of Jupiter's moon Io? NASA's Juno probe just took a peek
articles.excerpt: The subsurface findings could transform how scientists study volcanoes across the solar system — including those on Earth.
articles.body: Scientists have measured temperatures beneath the surface of Jupiter's volcanic moon Io, offering the first-ever glimpse of the hidden heat driving the most volcanically active world in the solar system. During close flybys of Io in late 2023 and early 2024, NASA's Juno spacecraft turned its Microwave Radiometer (MWR) instrument toward the moon, probing roughly six to 20 feet (two to six meters) beneath its surface. Originally designed to peer through Jupiter's thick clouds, the instrument instead revealed a powerful new way to study how heat moves through planetary crusts, according to a statement from the space agency.
articles.author: Samantha Mathewson

articles.title: NASA to Support Blue Origin New Glenn Rocket Testing, Advance Artemis
articles.excerpt: NASA and Blue Origin announced Friday they recently agreed to conduct second stage hot fire testing for the company’s New Glenn rocket on the B-2 test stand at the agency’s Stennis Space Center near B…
articles.body: An image shows the B-1 and B-2 test stands at NASA’s Stennis Space Center near Bay St. Louis, Mississippi, on July 7, 2026. The test stand features dual, vertical firing positions built in the 1960s to test Saturn V rocket stages that carried humans to the Moon during the Apollo Program. The B-1 position, right, is equipped for single engine tests, while the B-2 position is designed to test full rocket stages. NASA announced an agreement with Blue Origin on July 24, 2026, to use the B-2 test stand for engine testing to advance rocket development for future missions to the Moon under the Artemis program.
articles.author: Lauren E. Low

articles.title: Certighost Exploit Lets Low-Privileged Active Directory Users Impersonate a Domain Controller
articles.excerpt: Researchers H0j3n and Aniq Fakhrul published a working exploit on July 24 that lets a low-privileged Active Directory user obtain a certificate for a Domain Controller and authenticate as that machine…
articles.body: Researchers H0j3n and Aniq Fakhrul published a working exploit on July 24 that lets a low-privileged Active Directory user obtain a certificate for a Domain Controller and authenticate as that machine. They codenamed the flaw Certighost. Because Domain Controller accounts carry directory replication rights, the resulting Kerberos credential can retrieve the krbtgt secret through DCSync.
